strengths: Clear identification of three distinct real-world objects with contextualisation
Explicit connections to the IA prompt supported by specific quotations and references
Commentary offers plausible rationales for each objectâs inclusion, moving beyond mere description
weaknesses: Real-world contexts are occasionally vague or underdeveloped
Explanations sometimes rely on general assertions without deeper TOK concept analysis
Noticeable repetition in key terms dilutes analytical depth and precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition reliably meets the core expectations of the 5â6 band: it presents three recognisable objects situated in real-world contexts and explains their links to the chosen prompt with supporting evidence. Quotations from the poem and Qurâan and references to mathematical rules demonstrate tangible engagement. However, the work falls short of the highest levels due to underdeveloped contextual details, surface-level analysis of TOK concepts, and repetitive phrasing. Awarding 6 marks reflects solid performance that fulfils most requirements of the band while acknowledging areas for greater depth and precision.

annotations.comment: Using anecdotal peer interpretations as primary evidence undermines credibility; supplement with scholarly or theoretical sources on literary interpretation.
Reference to âChambers et al.â lacks full bibliographic details; ensure proper academic citations to validate claims about literary knowledge.
Including Umberto Ecoâs perspective demonstrates engagement with a recognized theorist on interpretation, adding theoretical weight to the analysis.
The prompt is clearly stated, framing the exhibitionâs focus on interpretation across disciplines and providing a coherent guiding question for all three objects.
The student identifies the poem but fails to situate it in a specific publication date or cultural context, limiting the depth of its real-world relevance.
Reproducing extensive poem excerpts without source citation (e.g., edition, line numbers) weakens academic rigor; include precise reference details.
Analysis remains surface-level, relying on general assertions rather than exploring TOK concepts like personal vs shared knowledge or role of language in interpretation.
The definition of âinterpretationâ is sourced from a general dictionary; integrate TOK-specific definitions or academic frameworks to strengthen conceptual grounding.
The discussion of human cloning cites unverified online sources (âCodingestâ) without critical evaluation; integrate recognized Islamic jurisprudence literature for accuracy.
There is noticeable repetition in phrasing (âopen to interpretationâ, âblack and whiteâ), which dilutes analytical depth; vary vocabulary and focus on precise distinctions.
Real-world contexts are underdevelopedâlink each object to a specific event, publication, or personal experience to strengthen the exhibitionâs relevance.
While disciplines vary, all objects focus on textual knowledge; consider including a non-textual object to broaden the exhibitionâs exploration of interpretation.
The exhibition lacks a concluding synthesis that ties the three examples back to the prompt holistically; include a brief conclusion to reinforce the overarching argument.
The source âRabatatu al-âAlam al-Islamiâ is ambiguously cited; provide full reference details and assess its scholarly credibility (e.g., author credentials).
The photograph appears to be a stock image; the student should include a personal photograph of their specific object to strengthen authenticity.
Referencing âMuhammad Hakimâ without contextualizing his scholarly standing limits authority; specify his expertise and the tafsir tradition he represents.
Identifying personal math notes grounds the object in a concrete learning context, showcasing personal engagement with mathematical knowledge.
Conflating the interpretation of methods with the interpretability of knowledge leads to conceptual confusion; clarify how TOK defines interpretation in mathematics.
Describing rules (chain, product, quotient) without a worked example misses an opportunity to illustrate how mathematical interpretation functions in practice.
The student clearly identifies the Quran as a historical object, specifying its revelation period and recognizing its significance as a shared religious text.
Quoting Surah Ali âImran (3:7) provides concrete evidence for discussion of interpretive authority, effectively linking religious text to the prompt.
Citing âRamchandaniâ without full details obscures the sourceâs validity; include complete citation and explain its relevance to mathematical universality.
The analysis would benefit from explicit references to core TOK concepts (e.g., shared vs personal knowledge, certainty, language) to deepen conceptual rigor.
The student repeatedly uses âopen to interpretationâ but does not differentiate between personal and shared knowledge frameworks, which is central to the TOK argument.
The justification for each object remains descriptive rather than critically evaluative; engage more with implications and counterarguments.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
